fragility_points: The estimate is fragile to one more failed breakout in the high-$2,390s.
Exact authoritative rules wording was not fully recoverable in this run's public fetch.
Extreme market pricing leaves little room for path failure.
key_assumptions: Continuation and ordinary crypto volatility matter more than any single scheduled catalyst for the final move to $2,400.
Polymarket settlement mechanics are touch-friendly enough that a brief qualifying high would count.
A failed breakout remains possible even when spot is only a few dollars below the threshold.
main_logical_chain: Start from the extreme market prior of 92.35% and treat it as a timing judgment that requires extra verification.
Check the contract surface to identify the governing rules source and confirm that market mechanics likely favor a touch interpretation.
Verify current ETH proximity with independent market-data sources and confirm the remaining distance is tiny.
Keep a high Yes probability because ordinary volatility can finish the move, but stay below market because no hard catalyst or perfectly clean rules extraction justifies near-certainty.
main_thesis: ETH is still likely to reach $2,400 this week because it is already within a few dollars of the threshold, but the market is pricing closer to inevitability than the identified catalyst set justifies.
source_quality_view: Good enough for a low-difficulty case: one primary contract surface plus independent market-state checks, but exact source-of-truth wording remains only medium-clarity.
strongest_disconfirmers: No specific hard catalyst was identified that makes a final break above $2,400 close to automatic.
A failed breakout or broad crypto risk-off move could still strand ETH just below the threshold.
strongest_supports: ETH was trading around $2,392 during the run, only a few dollars below $2,400.
Binance daily highs had already reached roughly $2,394.71-$2,396.03 in the active window.
The contract surface suggests a touch/high-style framing rather than a close-above framing.
timing_relevance: Very high; this is a short-dated threshold-touch market where current distance to the level matters more than slow-moving fundamentals.
unresolved_ambiguities: Exact venue/candle wording in the authoritative Polymarket rules text.
Whether current momentum continues or exhausts just below $2,400.
what_would_change_view: I would move up if exact rules proved especially permissive and ETH kept pressing fresh highs near $2,400, and move down if ETH rejected again or the rules proved more restrictive than assumed.
